text.title: Clean Architecture Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply Clean Architecture end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: name-layers-and-boundaries
step.action: Pin the four layers in the repo (`entities/`, `use_cases/`, `interface_adapters/`, `frameworks/`) and the boundaries between them. Add an import-direction lint (e.g. ruff TID, eslint no-restricted-imports).

step.name: write-entity-tests-first
step.action: For each entity, write the test suite with zero-IO assertion (r2). Add CI gate: entity test runtime ≤100ms total.

step.name: define-use-cases-with-ports
step.action: Each use case declares its ports (input/output interfaces) in the inner layer. Outer layers implement.

step.name: wire-frameworks-at-edge
step.action: ORM/HTTP/queue lives in `frameworks/`; the interface_adapters layer translates between framework types and use-case ports.

step.name: publish-events-after-persist
step.action: Either commit-then-publish or transactional outbox (r3). Add an integration test that simulates a crash between commit and publish.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
